issuesFound.fix: Line 310: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transform transition on hover, but the accompanying @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable the motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 632: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 240: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff ratio 3.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb) on the default white background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision will struggle to read text in this colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4: --colour-brand-primary: #2266aa;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Links are distinguished only by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close functions change visible state but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know if the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open function, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In mobileNav.close function, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close functions show/hide panels but don't update aria-expanded on trigger buttons. Screen reader users won't know dropdown state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance function, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In _closeInstance function, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons change visual state but don't set aria-pressed. Screen reader users won't know which option is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState function, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Product filter does not announce dynamic content updates
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product grid updates, no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the change. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 for status changes.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters function, after updating count, add: `liveRegion.announce(\`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers not initialized with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen reader users may not know initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In register function, after setting up trigger, add: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel does not announce slide changes
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slide changes update visual content but aren't announced to screen readers via live region. Users with visual impairments won't know content changed.
issuesFound.fix: In goTo function, replace existing announcement with: `liveRegion.announce(\`Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}: ${_slides[target].label}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce price updates
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period changes and prices update, no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the change. This violates WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod function, after updating cards, add: `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped inside the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the menu into background content, violating WCAG 2.1.1 for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: In open function, after opening, add: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. In close function, add: `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button does not update aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel play/pause button toggles state but doesn't update aria-label to reflect current action. Screen reader users won't know if pressing will play or pause.
issuesFound.fix: In play function, add: `_playBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Pause automatic slide rotation');`. In pause function, add: `_playBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Start automatic slide rotation');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation marks fields with CSS classes but doesn't set aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers won't announce invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. When valid, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When search suggestions load, no announcement informs screen reader users of result count. Users with visual impairments won't know suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: In _fetchAndRender function, after getting results, add: `liveRegion.announce(\`${results.length} suggestion${results.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available\`);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion open/close functions change visible state but don't update aria-expanded on trigger buttons. Screen reader users won't know if answers are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In open function, add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In close function, add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.explanation: The element `[data-billing-toggle]` acts as a two‑state switch (monthly ↔ annual) but the script never sets `aria‑pressed`. AT users cannot perceive the current state.

issuesFound.title: “Scroll‑to‑top” button has no accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button identified by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is only hidden/visible via CSS and has no text, `aria-label`, or `title`. Keyboard‑only users cannot discover its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Search input lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search `<input>` receives `aria‑autocomplete`, `aria‑controls`, etc., but no label, `aria-label`, or `title`. Users of assistive technology have no programmatic name for the search field.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the input is labelled, for example by adding a `<label>` in the markup or, if that is not possible, set an `aria-label` in script:

```js
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.searchLabel || 'Site search');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are not hidden from AT when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the script adds `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`. Some ATs still expose the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance` add:

```js
inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels are not marked with `role="region"` when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ panels receive `role="region"` on creation, but when a panel is hidden the script only adds `hidden` without also setting `aria‑hidden="true"`. This can cause AT to read hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close(item)` add:

```js
item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function (and the `open()` / `close()` helpers it calls) changes the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

In `mobileNav.toggle()` (or in the `open()` / `close()` functions) add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` after the drawer state changes.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` routine marks fields as invalid by adding an error message element, but it never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the offending input (or removes it when the field becomes valid). This prevents screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
When a field fails validation, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; when it passes, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')` (or set to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the focus outline (outline: none) without providing a visible alternative. Users in high-contrast mode will lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline in the focus-visible state without providing a visible alternative indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible removes the outline with no visible replacement. Users navigating dropdown menus via keyboard cannot see which link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus outline in focus-visible state without providing a visible alternative indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing which filter is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes focus outline without replacement. Keyboard users need to see focus when they tab into the scrollable region.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline with outline: none and provide no visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum 24px on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum 24px on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate when small.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum 24px on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes are sized at 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on mobile nav toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has a touch target of 28px × 28px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links and buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px, below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on standard buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The standard .btn class has a min-height of 32px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on large buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class has a min-height of 36px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on hero primary CTA
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button has a min-height of 32px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on hero secondary CTA
issuesFound.explanation: The hero secondary button has a min-height of 32px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on product card buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, below the 44px recommended target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended 44px on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users while also potentially breaking screen reader announcements. It fails to visually hide content.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties, which causes spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties (rotateY, rotateX) that creates spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 593 is empty and does not disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature card arrow
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link arrow (::after pseudo-element) has a transition on transform that moves the arrow horizontally. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform (translateY and scale) on hover that creates spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1129 only targets .pricing-card:hover, not .pricing-card--featured:hover.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and violates text spacing requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 93: 
  --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label uses var(--colour-neutral-400) which is #94a3b8 on a background of var(--colour-neutral-50) which is #f8fafc, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 24: 
  --colour-neutral-400: #6b7280;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Many links in the design (such as footer navigation and account navigation) rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer. However, these functions never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the current state of the drawer. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2808 (in open function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`. Line 2832 (in close function): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after setting `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, which manipulate the hidden attribute and CSS classes. Neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to indicate the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3105 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` before emitting the open event. Line 3125 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion opens and closes items via the open() and close() functions, which animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute. While initial ARIA states are set during init(), these toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button when the state changes dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4810 (in open function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after setting `item.open = true;`. Line 4835 (in close function): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle element is assigned role="switch" but the code only toggles CSS classes. A switch role requires aria-checked to be updated to reflect the state (true/false), but neither _onToggleClick nor _syncToggleState updates this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4165 (in _syncToggleState): After `const isYear = period === 'year';`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));`. Also ensure this is called in _onToggleClick after toggling the period.

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is never initialised. When the suggestion panel is closed (hidden), the combobox must have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the popup is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3830 (after setting role and aria-controls): Add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Additionally, update this to 'true' in the show() function and 'false' in the hide() function of suggestionListRenderer.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" even though the panel starts hidden. This violates the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080 (after setting aria-controls): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count is updated in the DOM via _updateCountBadge, but this change is not announced to screen reader users. Users navigating with assistive technology will not know that the filter has been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4545 (after _updateCountBadge call): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` to announce the results count.

issuesFound.title: Pricing price updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, the price text content is updated dynamically via textContent assignment. This visual change is not announced to screen reader users, who may not realise the price has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4120 (after updating priceDisplay.textContent): Add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} per ${period}`);` to inform users of the price change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, the trigger button is not set to aria-expanded="false" to indicate the menu starts in a collapsed state. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the button controls a collapsible region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185 (after `_initialised = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes but do not programmatically communicate the pressed state via aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1855 (inside forEach): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the aria-describedby reference to the error message but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815 (after removing describedby): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters and the product grid updates, the change in results count and active filter category is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140 (after _updateCountBadge): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));` or similar announcement using the existing STRINGS configuration.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770 (after updating prices): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing initial aria-expanded state on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element acts as a combobox but is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion list is closed on page load. This violates the ARIA combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955 (after setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox')): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open and _close functions in siteSearch manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox by setting _searchState.open and calling suggestionListRenderer.show/hide, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the combobox input (_inputEl). The input has role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", so it must reflect the popup's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2657: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2665: Add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function (called by inlineValidator._renderResult at line 3737) only manages the aria-describedby relationship to link the field with its error message. It does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Similarly, markValid does not clear aria-invalid. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1587: Add el.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); Line 1597: After removing describedBy, add el.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); or el.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register function in dropdownMenu sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger element, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" when the dropdown is first registered and the panel is hidden (line 2072). Screen readers cannot infer the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2073: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch mount does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function configures the search input as a combobox by setting role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but it fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" before the suggestion list is shown. The attribute is never initialized, leaving screen readers unaware of the popup's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function dynamically shows/hides product cards, updates the count badge, and displays empty states, but it does not announce these changes to screen readers via a live region. Users navigating with assistive technologies are not informed when filter results update or how many products match the current criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2786: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); or similar announcement after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers via a live region. While the aria-expanded fix (Issue 1) will help when focus is on the trigger, the significant UI change of the drawer appearing/disappearing should be announced to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); Line 2171: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions reveal or hide dropdown panels but do not announce these visibility changes to screen readers. Although focus is moved into the panel when opened, users should be informed that the dropdown has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2046: Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown expanded'); Line 2056: Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown collapsed');

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents assistive technology from announcing whether the submenu is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~980: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger buttons in the mobile navigation use aria-controls to reference the submenu but omit aria-expanded, leaving screen reader users unaware of the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button uses aria-controls to reference the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the panel is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~710: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field for errors but does not map this to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~590: <input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling error states but fails to set aria-invalid, preventing assistive technology from communicating the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~610: <textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for visual error indication but does not set aria-invalid, so screen reader users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~630: <select id={id} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Checkbox component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Checkbox component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the input element, leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~650: <input id={id} type="checkbox" aria-describedby={errorId} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} className="checkbox-input" {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Links marked as current (item.current) receive a visual modifier class but lack aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~980: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Current page links in the mobile navigation have a visual indicator but no aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and lacks an aria-label, leaving screen reader users with no information about the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1040: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown chevron icon (▲/▼) is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the arrow characters as part of the button label.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~980: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~350: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation uses <nav> without an aria-label, failing to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~980: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a <nav> element without an aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the navigation context.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: <nav aria-label="Main" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article element missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state

issuesFound.fix: Line ~450: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>
